vulnerabilities.description: Hardcoded secret value '#secretKey = "hidden-value"' in example class — although pragma-allowlisted and in a demo file, could be copy-pasted into production code
Hardcoded 'password: secret' in YAML config example — pragma-allowlisted but trains users to embed credentials in config files
Path traversal check uses naive string-based '..' check instead of resolving to canonical path — symlink-based traversal could bypass this
MCP server has no authentication or authorization — any stdio client can invoke all tools including file read/write, code execution via rg/fd
Subprocess execution (asyncio.create_subprocess_exec) with user-controlled command args — mitigated by using exec-form (no shell), but no input sanitization on query patterns
subprocess.run with user-derived PR slug and number — mitigated by list-form args (no shell=True), but slug from regex match has no allowlist validation
subprocess.run executes git log with pathspec derived from file_path — mitigated by list-form args, but pathspec could contain git rev-parse metacharacters
sanitize_input strips angle brackets and quotes — over-sanitization may break legitimate use cases and gives false sense of security for non-HTML contexts
Custom contextlib.suppress shim shadows stdlib contextlib — could mask errors silently if the shim diverges from stdlib behavior

recommendations: Add examples/ to .gitignore's security scanning exclusions or mark them clearly as non-production to prevent credential-pattern copy-paste
Replace string-based '..' path traversal check in code_file_resource.py with Path.resolve() comparison against allowed base directory
Add rate limiting or input validation to MCP server tool handlers — even stdio servers benefit from bounding query patterns and file counts
Sanitize ripgrep query strings before passing to subprocess — while exec-form prevents shell injection, special rg flags in query strings could change behavior
Validate PR slug format (alphanumeric + hyphens only) before passing to gh CLI subprocess in pr_url.py
Consider adding a security header/warning to the MCP server startup banner indicating no auth is enforced
Run 'bandit -r tree_sitter_analyzer/' and 'safety check' as CI gates — both are already in dev dependencies
